fresh_facts.claim: US military forces seized an Iran-linked ship (Tifani) in the Indian Ocean as part of the 'Economic Fury' campaign expanding the blockade of Iranian ports.
President Trump ordered the US Navy to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz.
Over 30 ships turned around due to the US blockade of Iranian ports.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the US military can transition from blockade to major combat operations.
Thousands of additional US forces are deploying to the region around the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran's IRGC gunboats attacked a container ship and cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on April 23, 2026.
IRGC commandos seized two cargo vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, April 23, 2026, as retaliation.
Iran shut down the Strait of Hormuz after briefly declaring it open, vowing continuation until the US blockade lifts.
Iran retains thousands of missiles and one-way attack drones despite prior US strikes depleting 70-90% of production capacity.
Israel's defense minister affirmed readiness to renew war and is awaiting US green light.
The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was extended for 3 weeks as of April 23, 2026.
Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf is pushing diplomacy while IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi opposes it, per an Institute for the Study of War assessment.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi has direct access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, giving him significant influence over policy decisions.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: A power vacuum from unclear supreme leadership is emboldening hardliners against US agreements.
Iranian officials were unable to contact Mojtaba Khamenei as of April 19, 2026, hindering negotiations.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: ISW's live tracker documented regime control strain from January 2026 protests, including framing protesters as 'terrorists,' foreign militia deployments (800-5,000 Iraqi fighters), and high security casualties.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Supreme Leader Khamenei issued a 'shoot to kill' order against protesters in January 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Internet blackouts and Basij militia deployments persist in Iran as of February 2026, though protests remain suppressed in the short term.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Exiled Reza Pahlavi dismissed negotiators as repression enablers in a press conference on or around April 23, 2026.
US prediction markets show Reza Pahlavi leadership odds at 4.5% YES by June 30, 2026.
Prediction markets show Iranian regime fall odds at 3.2% by May 31, 2026, unchanged after the 100-day crackdown mark.
Iran's ceasefire with the US was set to expire on April 22, 2026, amid deep divisions within Iranian leadership over peace talks.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: US strikes have hit 78% of Iranian targets including manufacturing facilities, missiles, and drone production sites.
Iran refused to participate in US-led peace negotiations being held in Pakistan.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian officials are unable to set a firm deadline for a US-Iran deal as negotiations stall.

regime_fall.current_status: Active military confrontation escalated to direct shoot-to-kill rules of engagement as US forces expand blockade operations (seizing Tifani in Indian Ocean, detaining 30+ ships) while Iran responds with Strait of Hormuz closures and vessel seizures. Internal Iranian leadership paralysis—Vahidi's military hardline versus Ghalibaf's diplomacy, compounded by inability to contact Mojtaba Khamenei and a broader succession vacuum—prevents unified response amid simultaneous economic hemorrhaging and residual protest suppression.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Iran shut down the Strait of Hormuz after declaring it open, vowing continuation until US blockade lifts, while IRGC commandos seized two cargo vessels as retaliation on April 23, 2026, marking direct escalation beyond blockade enforcement.
